content.problemStatement:
Johnny has gone on a short excursion to the island of Bitland, and found himself with plenty of time to spare for sight-seeing. As a matter of fact, he does really have any itinerary at hand. All he would like to do is to walk around a bit, without spending too much money in the process. 


The cities of Bitland are connected by a network of one way streets. If there exists a road leading directly from city A to city B, then there also exists a road directly from city B to city A. There maybe any number (possibly zero) of parallel roads between any two cities. However, it is possible to reach any city starting from any other city (possibly indirectly, going through other cities on the way).


 Now, the trouble is that all of the roads in Bitland are privately owned, and toll trolls have been hired to guard them and collect the dues. What's worse, the trolls are moody and will in some circumstances change the sum of the toll charged for using a road...


Johnny starts his sight-seeing tour from the capital of Bitland. He always leaves each city by the cheapest outgoing road, i.e. the one which currently has the lowest toll. However, as soon as Johnny has used the road from A to B, the toll troll in charge of it increases the toll for the road, setting it at exactly 1 Bitlandian dinar more than the toll currently being charged on the most expensive of the roads leaving city A. 


Johnny has been walking around the towns of Bitland for a while, and he is getting a little bored. Help him answer the following question: what is the toll he is going to pay when traversing the k-th road of his tour?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers: n, the number of towns in Bitland and m, the number of pairs of roads connecting them. Each of the m following lines contains four integers A B cAB cBA, denoting the identifier of city A, the identifier of city B, the initial toll on the road from A to B, and the initial toll on the road from B to A. The next line of input contains the number t of queries asked by Johnny, t <= 10^4. Each of the following t lines contains a single integer k (1 <= k <= 10^12).
content.outputFormat: For each of the values of k given at input, print a line containing the toll paid by Johnny when traversing the k-th road along his route.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph data structure to represent the cities and roads of Bitland. We can then use a priority queue to keep track of the cheapest outgoing road from each city. When Johnny traverses a road, we update the toll of the road and the priority queue accordingly. We can use dynamic programming to precompute the tolls for the first k roads and then use this information to answer the queries.
solution.timeComplexity: O(m + n log n) — the time complexity of building the graph and priority queue. O(t log n) — the time complexity of answering the queries.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(m + n) — the space complexity of storing the graph and priority queue.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
import heapq

# Define the structure for a road
class Road:
    def __init__(self, from_, to, toll):
        self.from_ = from_
        self.to = to
        self.toll = toll

    def __lt__(self, other):
        return self.toll < other.toll

def main():
    # Read the input
    n, m = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
    # Create a graph to store the roads
    graph = [[float('inf')] * n for _ in range(n)]
    # Read the roads and store them in the graph
    for _ in range(m):
        from_, to, toll = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
        from_ -= 1
        to -= 1
        graph[from_][to] = toll
    # Create a priority queue to store the roads
    queue = []
    # Insert the roads from the starting city into the priority queue
    for i in range(n):
        if graph[0][i] != float('inf'):
            road = Road(0, i, graph[0][i])
            heapq.heappush(queue, road)
    # Read the queries and answer them
    t = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    for _ in range(t):
        k = int(sys.stdin.readline())
        # Find the k-th road
        count = 0
        while count < k:
            road = heapq.heappop(queue)
            count += 1
            # Update the toll of the road
            new_toll = graph[road.from_][road.to] + 1
            # Insert the new road into the priority queue
            for i in range(n):
                if graph[road.to][i] != float('inf'):
                    new_road = Road(road.to, i, graph[road.to][i])
                    heapq.heappush(queue, new_road)
        # Print the toll of the k-th road
        print(queue[0].toll)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
